scope: UX-07 — first failure is not the visual focus (Audit Workspace only)
method: rendered TextMesh character sizes + presence of the named non-typographic signals, projected through the d7feb01/01864b4 capture-rig model. Proves the design contract is applied, not aesthetic quality.
hierarchyMetric: the primary conclusion's rendered character size divided by the largest competing label's size, required >= MinConclusionDominanceRatio, PLUS the accent rule and padding signals

signals.typography: conclusion is the single largest label; both titles step down to ContextTitleSize while a failure is on screen
signals.accentRule: a failure-red bar under the conclusion (border/surface, not colour alone)
signals.padding: ConclusionPadAbove/Below separate it from the field rows

before.note: '◆ FIRST FAILURE' at 0.030 was smaller than two 0.052 titles; the word appeared 4x at 4 sizes across 3 regions; the eye landed on the story title

assertions: first-failure state applies the primary role (size + rule)
calm states apply NO failure emphasis and keep both titles at full size
conclusion uses >= 2 non-colour signals and clears the dominance ratio
supporting context stays visible and subordinate; nothing hidden
conclusion stays in region, no wrap/truncation/overlap/clipping
hierarchy survives colour removal; d664873/d7feb01/01864b4 invariants unchanged; Chinese carries the same roles

outOfScope.UX-12: NOT resolved — only the Audit Workspace changed; Room Flat colour grammar untouched
